rationale: The citing paper is a conceptual editorial on writing “sticky” JMR articles and states it uses June 2008 issue papers as illustrations, while its core framework comes from Heath and Heath (2007), not from this new-product adoption study. The cited paper’s topic (RNP vs. INP intention follow-through) is substantively different and appears likely to be an in-issue example rather than a method/theory source for the editorial. With only a bare DOI match and no contextual quote indicating substantive reliance, this looks like an incidental mention.
